steps.title: Apply the triangle inequality
steps.content: For side lengths $6$, $12$, and $x$, the sum of any two sides must be greater than the third side.

steps.title: Find the bounds on x
steps.content: The fixed sides give the upper bound: $6+12\gt x$, so $x\lt18$. The smaller fixed side plus $x$ must exceed the larger fixed side: $6+x\gt 12$, so $x\gt 6$.

steps.title: Combine and choose
steps.content: Together, the possible lengths are $6\lt x\lt18$. That confirms C is correct.
